label: Got Questions?
heading: Frequently Asked Questions
subheading: Everything you need to know about working with Gwaltney's All Seasons Pressure Washing.

items.question: What areas do you serve?
items.answer: We primarily serve Wilson County, Davidson County, and Rutherford County in Middle Tennessee. We're based in Mt. Juliet and regularly work in Lebanon, Murfreesboro, Nashville, Hermitage, Smyrna, Lavergne, and surrounding communities. Not sure if we cover your area? Just give us a call at 615-418-7454.

items.question: Are you licensed and insured?
items.answer: Yes. Gwaltney's All Seasons Pressure Washing is fully licensed and permitted to operate in the City of Mt. Juliet and throughout our service area. We carry full general liability insurance, so you're fully protected on every job.

items.question: How do you handle wastewater runoff?
items.answer: We take environmental compliance seriously. We use drain berms to contain wash water runoff and exclusively use biodegradable, eco-friendly cleaning solutions. Our process is fully compliant with City of Mt. Juliet wastewater regulations — protecting your property, your neighbors, and the environment.

items.question: What chemicals do you use? Are they safe for my family and pets?
items.answer: We use only biodegradable, environmentally responsible cleaning agents. Our solutions are safe for your family, pets, and landscaping when properly applied. We always take care around your garden, flower beds, and outdoor living areas — rinsing thoroughly before and after application.

items.question: Will pressure washing damage my home or property?
items.answer: Not when it's done correctly — and that's our specialty. Before starting any job, we assess every surface and select the right pressure level and technique. Delicate surfaces like painted wood, stucco, vinyl siding, and older brick receive low-pressure soft-wash treatment. With 15+ years of experience, we know exactly how to protect your property while still delivering exceptional results.

items.question: How long does a typical job take?
items.answer: A standard driveway takes roughly 1–2 hours. A full house wash is typically 2–4 hours depending on size and condition. Commercial jobs vary based on scope. When we quote your job, we'll give you a time estimate so you can plan your day accordingly.

items.question: How often should I have my property pressure washed?
items.answer: For most homes in Middle Tennessee, once a year is ideal — typically spring before the heavy mold and mildew season. High-traffic concrete areas like driveways can benefit from twice a year. Commercial properties often schedule quarterly or bi-annual cleanings to maintain a professional appearance.

items.question: Do I need to be home during the service?
items.answer: Not necessarily. As long as we have access to an outdoor water spigot and the areas to be cleaned, we can often complete the work while you're away. We'll communicate before, during, and after the job — and you'll have our number if any questions come up.

items.question: How do I prepare for my appointment?
items.answer: It's simple: make sure we have clear access to all areas being cleaned, close windows and doors, and move any outdoor furniture, vehicles, or items you don't want cleaned. That's it — we handle everything else.

items.question: What forms of payment do you accept?
items.answer: We accept Venmo (@Brian-Gwaltney-11), cash, and check. Payment is due upon satisfactory completion of the job. We don't take payment until you're happy with the results.

items.question: Do you offer commercial pressure washing services?
items.answer: Absolutely. We work with business owners, property managers, HOAs, and commercial property owners throughout Wilson, Davidson, and Rutherford Counties. Building exteriors, parking lots, loading docks, storefronts, and fleet vehicles — call us for a free custom quote.

items.question: Why choose Gwaltney's over a larger company?
items.answer: Because when you hire Gwaltney's, you get Brian Gwaltney — the owner — on every single job. Not a subcontractor, not a new hire, not someone you've never met. Someone with 15+ years of experience who treats your property like his own. Honest pricing, reliable scheduling, and work you can be proud of. That's the Gwaltney's difference.
